name: Performance Improvement Plan
summary: Structured PIP arc that helps a struggling employee see expectations clearly, understand the gap, and walk into a credible improvement path. Designed to be respectful, specific, and outcome-focused — not punitive.

beats.name: Introduction
beats.description: Begin respectfully and set a constructive tone. Be clear about what this conversation is and isn't. The opening shouldn't catastrophize or sugarcoat — clarity is kinder than either.

beats.name: Set Expectations
beats.description: Detail the level of performance expected in the role. Specific behaviors, deliverables, and standards. Expectations the employee can't quote back are expectations they can't meet.

beats.name: Identify Problems
beats.description: Name the gap between expected and observed performance. Use specific examples and dates rather than impressions. Avoid characterizing the person; focus on the work product and the impact.

beats.name: Possible Solutions
beats.description: Suggest improvement strategies tailored to the gaps named above. Behavior changes, structural changes, support structures. Frame them as paths the employee can choose, not orders to follow.

beats.name: Offer Resources
beats.description: Make explicit what tools, coaching, training, or peer support is available. Resources demonstrate that the organization is investing in the outcome, not setting the employee up to fail.

beats.name: Motivate Change
beats.description: Connect improvement to what the employee values: their growth, their team, their career. Inspire action without minimizing the seriousness of the situation.

beats.name: Follow Up
beats.description: Schedule the check-ins. Frequency, format, criteria for progress. Predictable follow-up turns a PIP from a threat into a plan.

beats.name: Conclusion
beats.description: Close with confidence in the path forward and a clear restatement of what success looks like. End the meeting having both raised the bar and reaffirmed the relationship.
